name: Korean Hot Pot - Shin Sul Ro
recipeIngredient: 4 oz Lean Beef Rump Or Fillet
4 oz Lean Ground Beef Or Pork
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------
VINEGAR SOY DIPPING SAUCE
- 3/4 cup Light Soy Sauce
- 1/4 cup White Vinegar
- 1/4 cup Toasted White Sesame Seeds
- Ground
- 2 teaspoons Finely Chopped Green Onions
This cook-at-the-table one-pot dish is served with a biting vinegar soy sauce dip. The meat and vegetables are eaten first; then the stock, well flavored by the ingredients and enhanced with chili, is served as a soup with a sprinkling of diced onions.
Very thinly slice the liver, sprinkle with salt and pepper, and fry lightly in a little vegetable oil and sesame oil until colored and sealed on the surface. Set aside.
Boil the tripe for 8 minutes in lightly salted water; drain and cut into narrow strips.
Cut the beef into thin slices. Pound with a meat mallet or the side of a cleaver, adding salt, pepper, a few drops each of sesame oil and soy sauce. Form small meatballs with wet hands. Fry in a mixture of sesame and vegetable oils until lightly browned.
Peel and slice the carrot. Drain the mushrooms and remove the stems. Bring the stock to a boil in a suitable vessel at the center of the table. Add the meat, vegetables, and nuts, and simmer gently for about 15 minutes. Spoon into small bowls with the vinegar soy dip.
When the meat and vegetables have been eaten, add the finely shredded chili and green onions to the remaining stock and serve in soup bowls.
Vinegar Soy Dipping Sauce: Mix all ingredients. The sauce keeps for several days in the refrigerator without the green onions, one day with the green onions added.
